Description: Stores all data in RAM rather than traditional disk-based storage, operating entirely within memory for data access and manipulation.
Benefit: Extremely low latency ( $\approx$  100 ns access vs 100,000 ns for SSD) and massive throughput of millions of writes per second.
Data Type/Structure Support: Strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, bitmaps, hyperloglogs, geospatial indexes, and streams.
Complexity (Inferred): Low Implementation Complexity; High Performance. Eliminates disk I/O logic but requires careful memory management.

Description: Uses one main thread and an event loop to handle client requests in sequence, avoiding multiple threads for core request processing.
Benefit: Eliminates overhead from context switching, locking, and synchronization; ensures predictable execution and atomic operations.
Complexity (Inferred): Low Implementation Complexity; High Operational Performance. Simplifies debugging but limits parallelism to a single core.

Description: Leverages system calls to monitor multiple client connections (sockets) simultaneously and only executes read/write when data is ready.
Benefit: Handles thousands of concurrent TCP connections without blocking or wasting CPU/memory resources during network I/O waits.
Complexity (Inferred): Moderate Implementation Complexity; High Performance. Requires deep integration with OS-specific system calls.

Description: Allows clients to send multiple commands in a single batch without waiting for the response of each individual command.
Benefit: Significantly reduces network round-trip time (RTT) and can boost throughput by more than 10 times.
Complexity (Inferred): Low Implementation Complexity; High Performance. Effectively masks network latency bottlenecks.

Description: Executes complex scripts directly on the server side in a single execution cycle.
Benefit: Reduces network round trips for complex logic and ensures the entire script runs atomically.
Complexity (Inferred): Moderate Implementation Complexity; High Performance. Moves compute closer to data.

Description: Distributes data across multiple nodes and provides built-in mechanisms for synchronous or asynchronous data copying.
Benefit: Enables linear scaling of performance and manages larger datasets than a single node could hold.
Complexity (Inferred): High Implementation Complexity; High Scalability. Adds architectural overhead but solves hardware limits.

Description: Writes data to disk (Append Only File or snapshots) in the background or at intervals while the main thread continues command execution.
Benefit: Provides durability and disaster recovery without blocking the high-speed main execution loop.
Complexity (Inferred): Moderate Implementation Complexity; Moderate Performance Trade-off. Balances data safety against I/O overhead.
